commands.desc: Print the current working directory path
List files and directories in the current folder
List files in long format — shows permissions, size, and date
List all files including hidden ones (starting with .)

Force remove files without confirmation prompts
Display detailed status information about a file

Strip the filename, leaving just the directory path

Create hard or symbolic links between files

View file contents one screen at a time (basic pager)
Show the first lines of a file (default: first 10)
Show the last lines of a file (default: last 10)
Open the Nano text editor — beginner-friendly, keyboard shortcuts shown at bottom
Open the Vim text editor — powerful but has a steep learning curve
Print text or variable values to the screen
Format and print text — like echo but more precise control
Search for files and directories matching specified criteria
Quickly find files by name using a prebuilt database
Search text using pattern matching — essential for filtering output

Merge lines from multiple files side by side

Pattern scanning and powerful text processing language

Compare two sorted files line by line

Kill processes by name or other matching attributes

Run a command repeatedly at intervals and display live output
Measure exactly how long a command takes to execute
Show how long the system (Termux session) has been running

Transfer data to or from a URL — supports many protocols
Download files from the web non-interactively
Securely connect to and control a remote machine

Interactively transfer files over SSH with an FTP-like interface

Display or configure network interface settings (legacy)
Show active network connections and listening ports (legacy)
Modern replacement for netstat — shows socket statistics

Scan networks and hosts for open ports and running services
Termux's package management wrapper — the primary tool for installing software
The underlying APT package manager — equivalent to pkg in Termux
Refresh the list of available packages from all configured repositories

Uninstall a package (keeps config files)

Display detailed information about a specific package

Change the owner and/or group of a file or directory
Switch to another user account (commonly used to switch to root)

Show or set the system's hostname

Show total, used, and available disk space per filesystem
Display free and used system memory (RAM and swap)

List information about all available block (storage) devices
